content: | Selection Factor | What Should Be Verified | Why It Matters | |---|---|---| | Verification Point | Motor winding resistance for insulation integrity | A mismatch here can lead to weak growth, poor yields, wasted upgrades, or the wrong buying decision. | | Verification Point | Bearing play and noise | A mismatch here can lead to weak growth, poor yields, wasted upgrades, or the wrong buying decision. | | Verification Point | Fan blade balance and cleanliness | A mismatch here can lead to weak growth, poor yields, wasted upgrades, or the wrong buying decision. | | Verification Point | Current draw (amperage) of the motor | A mismatch here can lead to weak growth, poor yields, wasted upgrades, or the wrong buying decision. | | Verification Point | Dust and debris accumulation on fan blades and motor windings | A mismatch here can lead to weak growth, poor yields, wasted upgrades, or the wrong buying decision. | | Verification Point | Lack of lubrication on motor bearings | A mismatch here can lead to weak growth, poor yields, wasted upgrades, or the wrong buying decision. | | Verification Point | Loose electrical connections leading to arcing and overheating | A mismatch here can lead to weak growth, poor yields, wasted upgrades, or the wrong buying decision. | | Verification Point | Worn belts causing reduced efficiency and motor strain | A mismatch here can lead to weak growth, poor yields, wasted upgrades, or the wrong buying decision. |

table_title: Risk Signals and Safer Buying Decisions
content: | Risk Signal | What It Usually Means | Safer Decision Path | |---|---|---| | Non-sealed motor bearings in any greenhouse application (rapid failure due to moisture/dust) | The setup may not match the real growing condition. | Diagnose first, then decide whether replacement, adjustment, or maintenance is the better move. | | Fixed-speed fans in greenhouses with fluctuating temperature/humidity demands | The setup may not match the real growing condition. | Diagnose first, then decide whether replacement, adjustment, or maintenance is the better move. | | Proprietary fan or vent components with limited or expensive replacement parts | The setup may not match the real growing condition. | Diagnose first, then decide whether replacement, adjustment, or maintenance is the better move. | | Lack of clear, manufacturer-provided maintenance schedule or service instructions | The setup may not match the real growing condition. | Diagnose first, then decide whether replacement, adjustment, or maintenance is the better move. |
